message:
Migrate core async runtime to asupersync, fix crypto capacity bugs, and refactor telemetry context

The fcp-async-core runtime::Builder now wraps asupersync::RuntimeBuilder
instead of tokio, adding a thread-local RuntimeContext stack with RAII
guard so nested runtimes resolve correctly. The current_runtime_handle()
helper exposes the active handle for spawn-from-async scenarios.

fcp-crypto HKDF key derivation had incorrect Vec::with_capacity
preallocation sizes for zone-key (10→13 for "FCP2-ZONE-KEY"),
object-id-key (18→17 for "FCP2-OBJECTID-KEY"), session-key (20→13 for
"FCP2-SESSION-"), and mac-key (10→9 for "FCP2-MAC-") info strings.
These were harmless (Vec grows as needed) but now match the actual
byte lengths of the prefix constants.

fcp-protocol session key derivation was doing a redundant double-HKDF:
first hkdf_sha256_array to produce a PRK, then HkdfSha256::expand with
a different info. Simplified to a single HkdfSha256::new + expand_to_array
call with the composite info (session_id as salt, shared_secret as IKM,
nonces as info), which is the correct RFC 5869 usage.

fcp-telemetry context storage migrated from fcp_async_core::task_local!
(which relied on tokio task-locals) to an explicit HashMap<String, Vec>
keyed by asupersync task ID or thread ID. This ensures telemetry context
propagation works correctly under the asupersync runtime where tokio
task-locals are not available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
